title: Treasure in Heaven
subtitle: On the mountain, Christ teaches: lay up no treasure on earth, but in heaven

stillness: Let the world grow cheap to you.
Let every carnal love become vile.
Forget that you are in this world,
for you have carried your heart away
to those who live with God in heaven.
gospel: Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth,
and where thieves dig through and steal.
But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven,
where neither rust nor moth devours,
and where thieves do not dig through nor steal.
for it has pleased your Father to give you the kingdom.
Sell what you possess, and give alms.
behold: Attend to each word as if you stood present there.
Behold the Lord on the mountain,
teaching the perfect contempt of earthly things for God's sake.
He shows you the peril of hoarding treasure below:
the rust, which is the corrosion of gold and silver and every metal;
the moth, the worm, which gnaws at grain and wine and garments;
the thief, who carries off even the precious stone.
And He points to a safer place to store it:
heaven, where there is no age to wear it down,
no violence and no fraud to seize it.
consider: But woe to us, for we do the whole contrary!
What shall I say of that command, by which we are bidden not to hoard upon earth?
Few perhaps obey it.
The rest hear the precept as if its opposite were spoken to them,
as if it were said: hoard by every means upon the earth.
So, forsaking heaven, they cling to the earth,
and rave over the gathering of money.
It is folly to lay up your treasure where you must depart,
and not to send it ahead where you will dwell forever.
Place your substance there, where your homeland is.
The just refuse to build and hoard here,
where they know themselves to be pilgrims and strangers.
Love is the weight of the soul, carrying it wherever it is borne;
and the soul is more truly where it loves than where it gives life.
Would you know where you lay up treasure? Attend to what you love.
Would you know what you love? Attend to what you ponder.
Into as many parts is the heart divided as there are things it loves.
He who serves money is bound by present fetters and prepared for fetters to come.
Do not shut up your soul in your cheap purse with silver idols,
a soul that can never fly across to heaven weighed down with coins.
Whatever vice conquers a man, to that vice he is enslaved,
and there he has his heart, where he has his treasure;
and from this turning of the heart follows a turning away from God.
prayer: make me lay up no earthly gain upon the earth,
but the rewards of merit in heaven;
and because no one can serve two masters with contrary allegiance,
free me from the dominion and servitude of the world, the flesh, and the devil,
that I may look toward heavenly contemplations, not earthly ones.
Add to the stature of my nature a cubit of grace in the present,
and of glory in the time to come,
that I may consider the lilies of the field, the devout of the Church,
clothed in the brightness of the virtues,
rather than the rich of this age, hay to be cast into the furnace of hell.
Let me seek above all the kingdom of God and His justice,
that with the provision of temporal things, along the way of the virtues,
I may come to the kingdom of heaven. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Amor est animæ pondus portans eam quocumque ipse fertur; et anima verius est ubi amat, quam ubi animat.
latinJewel.english: Love is the weight of the soul, carrying it wherever it is borne; and the soul is more truly where it loves than where it gives life.
